explanation:
To determine the value of the given expression:

$ \sin^{-1}\left\{\cot\left(\sin^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{4}} + \cos^{-1} \frac{\sqrt{12}}{4} + \sec^{-1} \sqrt{2}\right)\right\} $

Step 1: Simplifying inside the arcsin and arccos

First, simplify $\sin^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{4}}$:

Rewrite under a common denominator:

$ \sqrt{\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{4}} = \sqrt{\frac{4-2\sqrt{3}}{8}} = \sqrt{\frac{(\sqrt{3}-1)^2}{8}} $

Simplify further:

$ \sin^{-1}\left(\sqrt{\frac{(\sqrt{3}-1)^2}{8}}\right) = \sin^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{2\sqrt{2}}\right) $

Given:

$ \sin^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{2 \sqrt{2}}\right) = \frac{\pi}{12} $

Step 2: Simplifying $\cos^{-1} \sqrt{3}/2$

Given:

$ \cos^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{12}}{4}\right) = \cos^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right) = \frac{\pi}{6} $

Step 3: Simplifying $\sec^{-1} \sqrt{2}$

Given:

$ \sec^{-1} \sqrt{2} = \frac{\pi}{4} $

Step 4: Substitute back into the expression

Substituting the simplified values:

$ \cot\left(\frac{\pi}{12} + \frac{\pi}{6} + \frac{\pi}{4}\right) = \cot\left(\frac{\pi}{12} + \frac{2\pi}{12} + \frac{3\pi}{12}\right) = \cot\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) $

Step 5: Evaluate the cotangent

$ \cot\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) = 0 $

Thus, the final expression becomes:

$ \sin^{-1}(0) = 0 $

The value of the expression is $0$.

content: The sum of the infinite terms of the series $\cot ^{-1}\left(1^2+\frac{3}{4}\right)+\cot ^{-1}\left(2^2+\frac{3}{4}\right)$ $+\cot ^{-1}\left(3^2+\frac{3}{4}\right)+\ldots$ is equal to
If $$\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)^2-\left(\cos ^{-1} x\right)^2=a \pi^2$$, then the range of $$a$$ is
explanation: $$\begin{aligned} & \text { } \because a \pi^2=\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)^2-\left(\cos ^{-1} x\right)^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad a \pi^2=\left(\sin ^{-1} x-\cos ^{-1} x\right)\left(\sin ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad a \pi^2=\left(\sin ^{-1} x-\cos ^{-1} x\right)\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 2 a \pi=2 \sin ^{-1} x-\pi / 2 \\ & \text { Now },-\pi \leq 2 \sin ^{-1} x \leq \pi \\ & \Rightarrow \quad-3 \pi / 2 \leq 2 a x<\pi / 2 \Rightarrow-3 / 4 \leq a \leq 1 / 4 \end{aligned}$$

$$3 \cos ^{-1} x-\pi x-\frac{\pi}{2}=0 \Rightarrow \cos ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi x}{3}+\frac{\pi}{6}$$

From the above graph, there is only one positive solution.

content: If $$\cot ^{-1}(y)=\cot ^{-1}(x)+\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{x^2-1}{2 x}\right)$$, then the value of $$y$$ is
explanation: $$\begin{aligned} & \text { } \cot ^{-1}(y)=\cot ^{-1}(x)+\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{x^2-1}{2 x}\right) \\ & =\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{x\left(\frac{x^2-1}{2 x}\right)-1}{x+\left(\frac{x^2-1}{2 x}\right)}\right)=\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{x^3-x-2 x}{2 x^2+x^2-1}\right) \\ & =\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{x^3-3 x}{3 x^2-1}\right) \Rightarrow \cot ^{-1}(y)=\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{x^3-3 x}{3 x^2-1}\right) \\ & \therefore \quad y=\frac{x^3-3 x}{3 x^2-1} \end{aligned}$$

content: If $$\tan ^{-1} y=\tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1-x^2}\right)$$, where $$|x|< \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}$$ then value of $$y$$ is
explanation: $$\begin{aligned} & \text {Given, } \tan ^{-1} y=\tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1-x^2}\right) \\ & \text { where }|x|<\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \\ & \Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} y=\tan \left\{\frac{x+\frac{2 x}{1-x^2}}{1-x\left(\frac{2 x}{1-x^2}\right)}\right\} \\ & \left[\because \tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1} y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+y}{1-x y}\right)\right] \\ & =\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x-x^3+2 x}{1-x^2-2 x^2}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \tan ^{-1} y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 x-x^3}{1-3 x^2}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad y=\frac{3 x-x^3}{1-3 x^2} \end{aligned}$$

content: If $$\cos ^{-1} x-\cos ^{-1} \frac{y}{2}=\alpha$$ where $$-1 \leq x \leq 1, -2 \leq y \leq 2, x \leq \frac{y}{2}$$, then for all $$x, y, 4 x^2-4 x y \cos \alpha+y^2$$ is equal to
explanation:
Given, equation is

$$\begin{aligned} & \cos ^{-1} x-\cos ^{-1} \frac{y}{2}=\alpha \text { where, }-1 \leq x \leq 1, -2 \leq y \leq 2 \text { and } x \leq \frac{y}{2} \\ & \therefore \cos ^{-1}\left(x \cdot \frac{y}{2}+\sqrt{1-x^2} \sqrt{1-\left(\frac{y}{2}\right)^2}\right)=\alpha \\ & (\because \cos ^{-1} x-\cos ^{-1} y=\cos ^{-1}(x y+\sqrt{1-x^2} \sqrt{1-y^2})\\ & \Rightarrow \frac{x y^{\prime}}{2}+\sqrt{1-x^2} \sqrt{1-\left(\frac{y}{2}\right)^2}=\cos \alpha \\ & \sqrt{1-x^2} \sqrt{1-(y / 2)^2}=\cos \alpha-\frac{x y}{2} \end{aligned}$$

On squaring both sides, we get

$$\begin{aligned} & \left(1-x^2\right)\left(1-\frac{y^2}{4}\right)=\cos ^2 \alpha+\frac{x^2 y^2}{4}-\frac{2 x y}{2} \cos \alpha \\ & \Rightarrow\left(1-x^2\right)-\frac{y^2}{4}+\frac{x^2 y^2}{4}=\cos ^2 \alpha+\frac{x^2 y^2}{4} -xy \cos \alpha\\ & \Rightarrow x^2+\frac{y^2}{4}-x y \cos \alpha=1-\cos ^2 \alpha \\ & \Rightarrow 4 x^2-4 x y \cos \alpha+y^2=4 \sin ^2 \alpha \end{aligned}$$

content: Using the principal values, the value of $$\sin ^{-1}\left\{\sin \frac{5 \pi}{6}\right\}+\tan ^{-1}\left\{\tan \frac{\pi}{6}\right\}$$ is equal to

explanation:
$$\begin{aligned} & \text { } \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{5}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{5}\right) \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & =\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\frac{3}{4}+\frac{3}{5}}{1-\frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac{3}{5}}\right\}=\tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{\frac{15 \times 12}{20}}{\frac{20-9}{20}}\right\} \\ & =\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{27}{11}\right) \end{aligned}$$
